data.title: Update-site skill: artifact size tagging (XL, L, M, S)

data.body: Extend the /update-site skill pipeline so that when artifacts are created, they get tagged with a display size: XL, L, M, S. This size metadata feeds into studio desk layouts — larger sizes get more visual weight (bigger cells, prominent placement). The skill should assign sizes based on content significance, image dimensions, or explicit human input.

Additional requirements from Julianna:
1. SIZE HIERARCHY: XL/L/M/S tags drive visual weight in layouts — not random. If more variations needed later, system is extensible.
2. ARTIFACT NAMING: Each artifact gets a short, referenceable ID so edits are easy. Something like 'artifact 3' or '0305-3' (date + sequence number within that day). When Julianna says 'edit artifact 1-3-5' it should be unambiguous which entry/image she means.
3. The naming system needs to work in conversation (human says 'fix artifact 5') AND in the codebase (slug or frontmatter field maps to the reference).
